The '''Ruusan system''' was a [[star system]]<ref name="Episode Gallery">{{SW|url=series/clone-wars/duel-of-the-droids-episode-gallery|text="Duel of the Droids" Episode Gallery}} {{C|Slide 4}}</ref> located in the [[Teraab sector]]<ref name="BMF 98" /> of [[the galaxy]]'s [[Mid Rim Territories|Mid Rim]]<ref name="Galactic Atlas" /> that contained the [[planet]] [[Ruusan]],<ref name="Trivia Gallery" /> and was located at square P-11 on the [[Standard Galactic Grid]].<ref name="TFABG" /> During the [[Clone Wars]] in [[22 BBY]],<ref name="Date">''[[Star Wars: Galactic Atlas]]'' places the events of the [[Star Wars: The Clone Wars (film)|''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' film]] and "[[Bombad Jedi]]" to [[22 BBY]]. As this episode takes place between those two pieces of media according to {{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-clone-wars-chronological-episodeorder|text=''Star Wars: The Clone Wars'' Chronological Episode Order}}, the events of the episode "[[Duel of the Droids]]" must also take place in 22 [['ABY-BBY' dating system|BBY]].</ref> one of the planet's three [[moon]]s was the site of [[Skytop Station]], a secret [[Confederacy of Independent Systems|Separatist]] [[listening post]].<ref name="Duel of the Droids" /> At another point during the conflict, Separatist and [[Galactic Republic|Republic]] forces fought a [[Battle in the Ruusan system|battle]] in the system, with the wreckage of several [[starship]]s involved remaining in the system until well into the [[Imperial Era]].<ref name="BH4">[[Bounty Hunters 4|''Bounty Hunters'' 4]]</ref>

In the time following the [[Battle of Hoth]], several [[bounty hunter]]s journeyed to Ruusan in search of [[Nakano Lash]] who was taking care of [[Cadeliah]], heir to the [[Mourner's Wail Syndicate|Mourner's Wail]] and [[Unbroken Clan]] syndicates.<ref name="Hunters 3">[[Bounty Hunters 3|''Bounty Hunters'' 3]]</ref> The hunters [[Beilert Valance]] and [[T'onga]] followed Lash and Cadeliah into the system's orbital wreckage where they clashed with [[Boba Fett]], who shot T'onga and left Lash for [[Death|dead]].<ref name="Hunters 4">[[Bounty Hunters 4|''Bounty Hunters'' 4]]</ref><ref name="Hunters 5">[[Bounty Hunters 5|''Bounty Hunters'' 5]]</ref> T'onga was healed back on Ruusan, where she recovered from her injury and was rescued by her wife [[Losha Tarkon]].<ref name="Hunters 9" /><ref name="Hunters 10">[[Bounty Hunters 10|''Bounty Hunters'' 10]]</ref>
